content:
# John Chapter 1 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_001_page_001.jpg, JHN_001_page_002.jpg

### The Word Became Flesh

1. In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

2. He was with God in the beginning.

3. Everything came into existence through him, and nothing that exists was made without him.

4. In him was life, and that life was the light of the human race.

5. The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has never extinguished it.

6. God sent a man named John.

7. He came as a witness to testify about the light, so that through him everyone might believe.

8. He himself was not the light — he came only to point to the light.

9. The true light that gives light to every person was coming into the world.

10. He was in the world, and the world was made through him, yet the world didn't recognize him.

11. He came to his own people, and his own people rejected him.

12. But to everyone who received him and believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God —

13. children born not of human bloodlines, nor of physical desire, nor of any man's decision, but born of God.

14. The Word became a human being and made his home among us. We saw his glory — the glory of the Father's one and only Son, full of grace and truth.

15. John testified about him, crying out, "This is the one I meant when I said, 'Someone is coming after me who is far greater than I am, because he existed long before me.'"

16. From his abundance we have all received one gracious gift after another.

17. For the law was given through Moses, but grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

18. No one has ever seen God. But the one and only God, who is himself God and is at the Father's side — he has revealed him.

19. This was John's testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, "Who are you?"

20. John stated clearly — he didn't hedge — "I am not the Messiah."

21. "Then who are you?" they asked. "Are you Elijah?" "No," he said. "Are you the Prophet?" "No."

22. "Then who are you? We need an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?"

23. John replied in the words of the prophet Isaiah: "I am a voice shouting in the wilderness, 'Clear the way for the Lord's coming!'"

24. Then the Pharisees who had been sent

25. pressed him further: "If you aren't the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet, then why are you baptizing people?"

26. John answered, "I baptize with water, but right here in your midst stands someone you don't recognize.

27. He will come after me, and I'm not even worthy to be his slave and untie his sandals."

28. This took place at Bethany on the east side of the Jordan River, where John was baptizing.

### Jesus, the Lamb of God

29. The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and declared, "Look! The Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

30. This is the one I was talking about when I said, 'A man is coming after me who is far greater than I am, because he existed long before me.'

31. I didn't know who he was, but I have been baptizing with water so that he might be revealed to Israel."

32. Then John gave this testimony: "I saw the Spirit descend from heaven like a dove and rest on him.

33. I didn't recognize him as the one, but when God sent me to baptize with water, he told me, 'The one on whom you see the Spirit descend and rest — he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.'

34. I saw this happen, and I testify that this is the Son of God."

### The First Disciples

35. The next day John was standing with two of his disciples.

36. As Jesus walked by, John looked at him and declared, "Look! The Lamb of God!"

37. When the two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.

38. Jesus turned around and saw them following. "What are you looking for?" he asked. They replied, "Rabbi" (which means "Teacher"), "where are you staying?"

39. "Come and see," he said. So they went with him and saw where he was staying, and they spent the rest of that day with him. It was about four in the afternoon.

40. Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, was one of these two who had heard John's testimony and followed Jesus.

41. The first thing Andrew did was find his brother Simon and tell him, "We've found the Messiah!" (which means "Christ").

42. He brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus looked at him intently and said, "You are Simon, the son of John. But you will be called Cephas" (which means "Peter" — "the Rock").

43. The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, "Follow me."

44. Philip was from Bethsaida, the same town as Andrew and Peter.

45. Philip went to find Nathanael and told him, "We've found the one Moses wrote about in the Law, and the prophets wrote about too! His name is Jesus, the son of Joseph, from Nazareth."

46. "Nazareth!" Nathanael exclaimed. "Can anything good come from there?" "Come and see for yourself," Philip replied.

47. As Nathanael approached, Jesus said, "Now here is a genuine Israelite — a man without an ounce of deceit."

48. "How do you know me?" Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, "Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree."

49. Nathanael replied, "Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!"

50. Jesus said, "You believe just because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You will see far greater things than this."

51. Then he said, "I tell you the solemn truth: you will see heaven opened and the angels of God going up and coming down on the Son of Man."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 1 — "In the beginning was the Word":** John opens his Gospel with the same three words as Genesis: "In the beginning." But while Genesis says "God created," John says "was" — the Word already existed. Before creation began, the Word was already there. This is not the birth of God's self-expression — it is the eternal existence of it.

**Verse 14 — "The Word became flesh":** The most staggering sentence in the Bible. The eternal Logos — through whom the universe was made — became a human being. The Greek "tabernacled" (ἐσκήνωσεν) echoes the tabernacle in the wilderness where God's presence dwelt. God doesn't just visit — he moves in.

**Verse 18 — "The only-begotten God":** The Sinaiticus reads "the only-begotten God" — not "the only Son." This is the boldest christological statement in the NT. Jesus isn't just God's Son who reveals God. He is himself God — the one and only — who comes from the Father's side to make the invisible God visible. The change to "Son" in later manuscripts was probably an attempt to soften what seemed like an impossibly bold claim.

**Verse 29 — "The Lamb of God":** This title exists only in John. It fuses three images: the Passover lamb whose blood saves from death (Exodus 12), the lamb of Isaiah 53 who silently bears the people's sins, and the daily sacrifice at the temple. Jesus is all three at once.

**Verse 42 — "You will be called Cephas":** Jesus renames Simon at their first meeting. Κηφᾶς (Cephas) is Aramaic for "rock" — Πέτρος (Peter) in Greek. In the ancient world, renaming was an act of authority. Jesus sees not who Simon is, but who he will become.

**Verse 51 — "Angels ascending and descending on the Son of Man":** A direct allusion to Jacob's ladder (Genesis 28:12). In Genesis, angels go up and down on a ladder between heaven and earth. Jesus replaces the ladder — he is the connection point between heaven and earth. Where Jesus is, heaven is open.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
